steps.title: Identify the defining contrast
steps.content: The colon reveals the explanation: historians “had so much admiration” but were “far from objective.” The blank describes an attitude combining admiration with bias.

steps.title: Match the word to the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to name the attitude that combined such deep admiration that it prevented objectivity. Substitute each choice:
  • "reverence": "approach their subject with reverence" — reverence means deep, worshipful admiration. So much admiration that they lost objectivity fits perfectly. ✓
  • "candor": "approach their subject with candor" — candor means honesty and openness. That would make them more objective, not less. ✗
  • "prudence": "approach their subject with prudence" — prudence means careful good judgment. This has nothing to do with admiration-driven bias. ✗
  • "curiosity": "approach their subject with curiosity" — curiosity means a desire to learn. While plausible for historians, curiosity doesn't explain why they had "so much admiration" or lost objectivity. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
